title: Dapr Memory Session
description: Storing and retrieving agent conversation state via the Dapr state store, with ETag-based optimistic concurrency conflict handling

constructors: from_address(session_id, *, state_store_name, dapr_address='localhost:50001', session_settings=None, **kwargs) -> DaprSession  [classmethod, creates+owns DaprClient]

get_items: DaprSession: apply limit slice → messages[-session_limit:]  (if session_limit not None)
DaprSession: for each msg → _deserialize_item(msg) → TResponseInputItem  (if msg is str)
add_items: DaprSession: guard — return early if items is empty
DaprSession: for each item → _serialize_item(item) → json.dumps(item) → serialized_items: list[str]
DaprSession → DaprClient.get_state(store_name, key=messages_key, state_metadata=_get_read_metadata()) → StateResponse(data, etag)
DaprSession → DaprClient.save_state(store_name, key=messages_key, value=messages_json, etag=etag, state_metadata, options=StateOptions(first_write)) → success/exception
DaprClient → DaprStateStore: gRPC SaveState with ETag
DaprStateStore → DaprClient: OK  [ETag matches → write succeeds, break loop]
→ _is_concurrency_conflict(error): checks gRPC code (ABORTED|FAILED_PRECONDITION) or message markers
→ if attempt >= 5: return False → raise exception
DaprSession: build metadata dict {session_id, created_at, updated_at}
DaprSession → DaprClient.save_state(store_name, key=metadata_key, value=json(metadata), state_metadata, options=_get_state_options())
DaprClient → DaprStateStore: gRPC SaveState (metadata, no ETag/concurrency check)
pop_item: DaprSession → DaprClient.get_state(store_name, key=messages_key, state_metadata) → StateResponse(data, etag)
DaprSession: if messages empty → release lock, return None
DaprClient → DaprStateStore: gRPC SaveState with ETag
ON conflict exception → _handle_concurrency_conflict(error, attempt) → retry or raise
DaprSession: if last_item is str → _deserialize_item(last_item) → TResponseInputItem
ping: DaprSession → DaprClient.get_state(store_name, key='__ping__', state_metadata) → OK: return True

concurrency_conflict_handling._is_concurrency_conflict: Checks gRPC status code name in {ABORTED, FAILED_PRECONDITION} OR message contains: 'etag mismatch', 'etag does not match', 'precondition failed', 'concurrency conflict', 'invalid etag', 'failed to set key', 'user_script'
concurrency_conflict_handling._calculate_retry_delay: base = 0.05 * 2^max(0, attempt-1), capped at 1.0s, plus uniform jitter in [0, 10% of base]
concurrency_conflict_handling._handle_concurrency_conflict: Returns True (should retry) if: is_concurrency_conflict AND attempt < 5. Sleeps for calculated delay before returning True. Returns False to propagate exception otherwise.
concurrency_conflict_handling.strategy: Optimistic concurrency via ETag + Concurrency.first_write. Read-modify-write with up to 5 retries and exponential backoff with jitter.

key_relationships: DaprSession owns/wraps DaprClient (gRPC async client to Dapr sidecar)
DaprClient communicates with DaprStateStore via Dapr sidecar gRPC
DaprSession uses asyncio.Lock for mutual exclusion on all state operations
DaprSession depends on SessionSettings (via resolve_session_limit) for history limit
from_address() factory creates DaprClient internally (_owns_client=True); direct constructor accepts injected client
State stored under two keys: '{session_id}:messages' (list of serialized items) and '{session_id}:metadata' (timestamps)
important_notes: ETag-based optimistic locking: save_state uses Concurrency.first_write to detect conflicts
Consistency levels: 'eventual' (default) or 'strong' — passed via state_metadata for reads, StateOptions for writes
TTL: optional, passed as metadata 'ttlInSeconds' — support depends on backing store
Messages stored as JSON array of serialized strings; metadata stored as separate key
asyncio.Lock ensures no concurrent reads/writes within same DaprSession instance
close() only closes DaprClient if _owns_client=True; supports async context manager (__aenter__/__aexit__)
Used by Runner to load history before each turn and persist new items after each turn
